acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Target DAG documented
- [ ] Forbidden edges listed
- [ ] Policy references real crate names
description:
**Problem**
Boundary assumptions are implicit and drifting across crates.

**Files:** Cargo workspace metadata, architecture docs
design: Define allowed edges and forbidden edges as explicit policy. Align crate manifests with policy and add a one-page reference for contributors.
title: Publish target DAG + forbidden dependency edges

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Retryable push race coverage runs through the `bd migrate` CLI surface and asserts both convergence and terminal retry exhaustion.
- [ ] Migration integration coverage asserts blank lines are ignored without warnings.
- [ ] Migration integration coverage asserts duplicate logical edge rows collapse to one migrated edge.
- [ ] The new tests fail if CLI contention handling or row-handling contracts regress.
description:
**Problem**
The migration suite still exercises retryable push races through a direct sync test helper instead of the `bd migrate` CLI surface, and it does not yet pin two locked row-handling contracts from the hard-cutover plan: blank legacy deps lines should be ignored without warnings, and duplicate logical edge rows should collapse to one migrated edge.

**Files:** `crates/beads-rs/src/cli/backend.rs`, `crates/beads-git/src/sync.rs`, `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/migration.rs`
design:
**Design**
Add a test-only CLI contention hook and use it to move the retry/race coverage onto the actual `bd migrate` surface, then add end-to-end assertions for blank-line and duplicate-edge handling.

- Provide a test-only migration contention hook gated by the existing test environment so CLI integration tests can deterministically flip the remote store ref before push.
- Add CLI migration tests for both retry convergence and retry-budget exhaustion using that hook.
- Add an end-to-end migration test that feeds blank lines and duplicate legacy dep rows through `bd migrate to 1`, asserting no warnings for blanks and exactly one migrated edge for duplicates.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Final audit on 2026-03-11 against /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs-legacy-deps-v1-cutover @ b662e632: retryable push-race coverage now runs through the actual `bd migrate` CLI surface via a test-only hook gated by BD_TESTING, covering both convergence and retry-budget exhaustion. The migration suite also now pins the locked row-handling contracts that blank legacy deps lines are ignored without warnings and duplicate logical edge rows collapse to one migrated dependency. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check; just dylint; cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings; cargo test; cargo test -p beads-rs migration --features slow-tests -- --nocapture. --repo /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs

title: Migration CLI coverage should pin contention behavior and legacy blank-line or duplicate-edge handling
Add git wire sparse _v and WireBeadFull JSON roundtrip tests

description:
**Problem**
Durability/idempotency model fixes are not covered by the normal test suite, so regressions (e.g. non-monotonic client-observed receipts) can slip in.

**Design**
- Add a regression test under beads_stateright_models/tests that executes the durability idempotency ActorModel to completion and asserts all properties.
- Expose the example's build_model() so the test can call it without duplicating logic.
- Keep the test deterministic (single thread) and fail fast if model checking does not complete.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Test added in beads_stateright_models/tests that runs the durability idempotency model and asserts properties.
- [ ] Example exposes a callable build_model() for the test.
- [ ] cargo test in beads_stateright_models passes.

**Files:** beads_stateright_models/examples/durability_idempotency_machine.rs, beads_stateright_models/tests/
title: Stateright regression test for durability idempotency model

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Mid-file corruption retains all valid prefix frames in active history
- [ ] Repair outcomes are explicit enum states; whole-file quarantine only for truly unrecoverable cases
- [ ] Integration test covers large segment with single bad interior frame and verifies prefix survivability
description:
**Problem**
`fsck --repair` quarantines an entire segment on mid-file corruption, discarding all valid prefix records in that file.

**Files:** `crates/beads-daemon-core/src/wal/fsck.rs`, `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/wal/fsck.rs`
design: Replace whole-file quarantine for mid-file corruption with prefix salvage: materialize a repaired segment containing only verified prefix frames, then atomically swap/index it. Model repair outcomes with explicit typed variants that distinguish tail-truncate, prefix-salvage, and unrecoverable.
title: Fsck mid-file repair should salvage valid prefix
